definition: Private credit deploys ~$200-300B/yr from committed capital pools with 5-7 year fund lifecycles, creating a leak in the credit creation function that weakens monetary policy transmission. A 150bp rate hike does not stop a fund with $5B dry powder and a 3-year deployment window.
evidence: Direct lending funds deploy from 5-7 year committed capital
Deployment pace driven by fund lifecycle not Fed funds rate
pair_1 confirmed as original and important
Private credit is still smaller than bank + capital markets combined — dampening overall magnitude
open_questions: Can the Fed compensate by being more aggressive on rates?
Does rate insensitivity cut both ways — are rate cuts also less stimulative?
What share of total credit creation does rate-insensitive private credit represent?
